<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 6 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- Isolated files — no import edges between 6 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- Packaging/ assets (Info.plist, entitlements, icon) carry no Swift import edges and are merged into this zone by proximity; excluding non-Swift assets from community detection inputs would surface the overlay triad as its own coherent zone.
- OverlayController (service), OverlayView (component), and OverlayWindow (component) form a coherent presentation triad — once packaging files are excluded they should cluster cleanly with high internal cohesion.
- Verify OverlayWindow uses a non-activating NSPanel subclass appropriate for a bedtime overlay; the co-location of the entitlements file here is a useful reminder that the overlay's window level and focus behavior must match declared entitlements.
- Non-Swift packaging assets are merged into the overlay UI zone by community detection, masking the true cohesion of the OverlayController/OverlayView/OverlayWindow triad.
- OverlayController temporarily mutates NSApp's activation policy from accessory to regular as a side effect of presenting the overlay — this is a global app-state change that is invisible to callers and could interfere with other components that assume the policy is stable.
- The OverlayController → OverlayWindow → OverlayView chain passes Schedule directly down through all three layers without a ViewModel or display adapter, meaning any change to Schedule's structure will propagate as a compile-time break through the full overlay stack — this is intentional rigidity but should be documented.
- NSApp activation policy is switched inside OverlayController.present() as an undocumented side effect — callers have no way to opt out or observe the transition, creating hidden global state coupling.

</insights>
